pattern: Two entries can only shake hands through a shared prime factor, so give every prime its own hub node and union each index with the hubs of its own prime factors -- the whole array is one traversable group exactly when a single union-find pass collapses everyone into one root.
hints: Two elements are directly connectable only when they share some prime factor greater than 1 -- comparing every pair's gcd directly is far too slow to be the real approach.

Give each distinct prime its own 'hub' node and union every array index with the hubs of its own prime factors using a disjoint-set structure -- elements sharing a prime automatically land in the same set.
After processing all the numbers, check whether every index shares the same root as index 0. Also watch for the value 1: it has no prime factors at all, so it can never connect to anything else.

edge_inputs.description: a short chain that fully connects through shared factors
one element shares no prime with the rest
a slightly longer fully connected group
two large values near the constraint ceiling that share no factor
the value 1 is isolated even though the rest of the array is connected
a chain of powers of the same prime
two internally-connected groups that never touch each other

stress.description: random values across the full range, large enough to exercise the sieve and union-find at scale
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 15000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
